sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see that only one square moved - to [[26, 28], [26, 29], [27, 29], [27, 28]] which is the left position. But the other two clicks didn't move the remaining squares to the top and right positions. This is because each click moves the NEAREST square, and all 3 clicks moved the same square (the one that was closest to all 3 target positions).
Looking at the current state:
- One square at [[26, 28], ...] (left of flower)
- One square at [[35, 16], ...] (far from flower)
- One square at [[55, 46], ...] (far from flower)
- One square at [[56, 15], ...] (far from flower)
Wait, but the square that was at [[31, 32], ...] (bottom of flower) is no longer there! It seems like all 3 clicks moved the same square (the one that was at [[31, 32], ...]) to different positions. The final position is [[26, 28], ...] which corresponds to the last click at (27, 29).
So the issue is that all 3 clicks targeted the same square because it was the nearest one. I need to move each square individually, one click per square.
Let me reconsider the approach. I need to move 4 squares to 4 positions around the flower. Each click moves the nearest square to the clicked position. So I need to click positions that are nearest to the specific square I want to move.
Current squares:
1. [[26, 28], [26, 29], [27, 29], [27, 28]] - already at left position
2. [[35, 16], [35, 17], [36, 17], [36, 16]] - far left
3. [[55, 46], [55, 47], [56, 47], [56, 46]] - far right
4. [[56, 15], [56, 16], [57, 16], [57, 15]] - far bottom-left
I need to move squares 2, 3, and 4 to the top, right, and bottom positions around the flower. But first, let me reconsider whether the cardinal direction placement is correct. The level didn't complete after placing squares at top, left, and right (even though only one square ended up at left).
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal is not to place squares at cardinal directions. Let me look at the image again. The square at [[26, 28], ...] is now on the left side of the flower, partially overlapping it. 
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. The first square I moved to [[31, 32], ...] was at the bottom of the flower. Then I clicked 3 more positions, but they all moved the same square (the one at [[31, 32], ...]) because it was nearest to all 3 click positions. The final position is [[26, 28], ...] which is the left side.
So now I have:
- 1 square at left of flower
- 3 squares still far away
I need to move the remaining 3 squares to the other 3 positions (top, right, bottom). But I need to click positions that are nearest to each specific square.
Let me calculate distances:
- Square at [[35, 16], ...] (center ~35.5, 16.5):
  - To top (23, 33): distance ~12.5 rows, ~16.5 cols
  - To right (27, 38): distance ~8.5 rows, ~21.5 cols
  - To bottom (32, 33): distance ~3.5 rows, ~16.5 cols
- Square at [[55, 46], ...] (center ~55.5, 46.5):
  - To top (23, 33): distance ~32.5 rows, ~13.5 cols
  - To right (27, 38): distance ~28.5 rows, ~8.5 cols
  - To bottom (32, 33): distance ~23.5 rows, ~13.5 cols
- Square at [[56, 15], ...] (center ~56.5, 15.5):
  - To top (23, 33): distance ~33.5 rows, ~17.5 cols
  - To right (27, 38): distance ~29.5 rows, ~22.5 cols
  - To bottom (32, 33): distance ~24.5 rows, ~17.5 cols
So for the top position (23, 33), the nearest square is at [[35, 16], ...].
For the right position (27, 38), the nearest square is at [[55, 46], ...].
For the bottom position (32, 33), the nearest square is at [[35, 16], ...].
Wait, but I already moved the square at [[35, 16], ...] to the top. Let me reconsider.
Actually, I should move one square at a time and verify. Let me first move the square at [[35, 16], ...] to the top position (23, 33).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Only one square moved because all 3 clicks targeted the same nearest square.
# I need to move squares one at a time. Let me check current state and plan carefully.

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current magenta squares on board:")
squares = []
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'M' and node['pixels'] == 4:
        # Skip panel square
        if node['boundary'][0][0] > 5:
            squares.append(node['boundary'])
            print(f"  {node['boundary']}")

# I need to move 3 remaining squares to top, right, bottom of flower
# Flower center ~ (27, 33)
# Target positions (bottom-right of each 2x2 square):
# - Top: (23, 33)
# - Right: (27, 38)  
# - Bottom: (32, 33)

# First, move the square at [[35, 16], ...] to top (23, 33)
# This square is at rows 35-36, cols 16-17, so it's nearest to (23, 33) among the far squares
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 23, 'col': 33}])
print(f"\nMove to top: level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Magenta squares after:")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['color'] == 'M' and node['pixels'] == 4:
        if node['boundary'][0][0] > 5:
            print(f"  {node['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
